response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer, but his profile does not match this role's hard requirements. The position demands native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, and hands-on Linux kernel source modification for embedded LAN-switch security features; his listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German, and his career centers on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no evidence of networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), L2/L3 switching, or embedded firmware ownership.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Some professional C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus Linux and Docker familiarity
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and active knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a non-negotiable requirement for this role
No demonstrated Linux kernel, MACsec/802.1X, L2/L3 networking, or switch/firmware experience
Recent career is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than embedded C development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Broad senior engineering experience with minor C exposure
